title: 🦞 OpenClaw Tip #2: Set Up Cron Jobs for Automated Daily Tasks
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw Tip #2: Set Up Cron Jobs for Automated Daily Tasks. OpenClaw cron job automation - futuristic clock mechanism with digital circuits. One of OpenClaw’s most powerful features is its built-in **cron job scheduler**. ## How OpenClaw Cron Jobs Work. Cron jobs run inside the Gateway process, not the AI model itself. Jobs are persisted at `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`, so they survive restarts. ## Creating Your First Cron Job. --system-event "Reminder: review the weekly report" \. ## Useful Cron Job Ideas. ## Managing Cron Jobs. # Run a job manually. openclaw cron run <job-id>. openclaw cron runs --id <job-id>. # Delete a job. openclaw cron delete <job-id>`. **Pro tip:** Schedule resource-intensive cron jobs during off-peak hours (late night) to avoid hitting API rate limits during your active working hours. ##### Share this post. ##### Company AI Operations Need Ontology: It Is Not Just IT’s Job. In recent years, enterprises have rushed into artificial intelligence (AI) hoping to boost efficiency, optimize.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide | MI - Meta Intelligence
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide. Diagram of OpenClaw Cron scheduled task automation execution. If you're familiar with Linux's `crontab`, OpenClaw's Cron is conceptually similar -- it automatically executes tasks at specified times. But the key difference is: traditional crontab executes fixed Shell commands, while OpenClaw's Cron executes **natural language instructions**, with the AI agent autonomously deciding how to complete them. If a Cron task's execution time exceeds this value, the Gateway will forcibly terminate the connection, causing the task to be truncated -- the agent may have completed 80% of the work, but cannot return results due to the timeout. Cron is the key feature that transforms OpenClaw from a "passive tool" into a "proactive assistant." Once configured, the agent automatically executes tasks and reports results at your specified times -- you don't need to remember what to check, don't need to open a terminal, and don't need to be at the computer.
